evidence: Person missing recommended: jobTitle, sameAs, hasCredential
Entity of type FAQPage has no @id — cross-page interconnection blocked
FAQPage schema has 7 Questions but page shows ~14 FAQ pairs
Author has no sameAs URLs (should link LinkedIn + at least one other profile)
Author has no jobTitle or description — credentials not stated
No first-party data signals ("we tested", "our data shows", "we analyzed"). Add at least one concrete observation
Only 3/4 human signals present (author+LinkedIn=false, first-party data=false, original visual=true, 3+ citations=true). High AI-content-flag risk
Specific human voice vs committee 4/10 — Mix of branded committee-speak and inserted 'voice' lines that feel bolted on by different authors.
Genuine opinions vs safe claims 6/10 — Some sharp opinions exist but are drowned in hedged, guideline-citing sentences.
Unexpected phrasings 6/10 — Good moments ('shelf life of 78 days', '45°C August') mixed with generic wellness phrasing.
Quotable sentences 6/10 — A few quotable lines exist but drown in boilerplate.
Intro earns attention 5/10 — The TL;DR and opening line are flat; the killer 78-days stat is buried paragraphs down.

specificity 6/10 — The coaching section collapses into abstractions: 'social and professional reinforcement,' 'strategies adapted to your reality,' 'generic advice.' The rest of the post earns specificity points with named figures (AED 1,200, HbA1c, 19 March 2026, 45°C, February humidity), but the 'when to seek coaching' and 'long-term well-being' sections drift into vague motivational language without named services, durations, costs, or measurable triggers. The worst offender is the coaching paragraph, which promises local expertise but delivers no concrete example of what that expertise looks like.
